Patient: I guess I could say something like... "I'll make sure to stay outside with the rest of the team if we're all hanging out and someone's smoking." That way, I'm still doing what I want to do, which is play cricket and hang out with my friends, but I'm not giving in to the temptation to smoke.
Therapist: You've figured out a way to handle the situation when your teammates are smoking - how do you plan to make sure you actually stay outside with the team in that situation?

Patient: I don't know... maybe I could ask one of my friends who doesn't smoke to hang with me outside, or just make a point to go for a walk around the block if I'm feeling really tempted. My coach said something about how quitting is a process, and that it's okay to slip up sometimes... so I guess I'm just taking it one day at a time.
